verses.text: These are the statutes ye shall observe in the land. Utterly destroy all the places of worship of the nations — on high mountains, hills, under every green tree. Overthrow their altars, break their pillars, burn their groves, hew down graven images, destroy the names out of that place. Guidance: Israel does not move into the Canaanite house and keep the family shrine. Holiness starts with demolition. God names where the gods lived — peaks, hills, leafy trees — and tells His people to leave nothing standing. Even the names get scrubbed. Some lines must be drawn with fire.

Ye shall not do so unto the LORD your God. But unto the place which the LORD shall choose to put His name there, thither shalt thou come. Bring your burnt offerings, sacrifices, tithes, heave offerings, vows, freewill offerings, firstlings. There ye shall eat before the LORD your God, rejoice in all that ye put your hand unto, ye and your households. Guidance: One place, one name, one rejoicing. The God who refuses high-hill plurality also refuses His own plurality. The meal is family-shaped — the whole household at the LORD's table, joy in the giver of harvests.

Ye shall not do as we do here, every man whatsoever is right in his own eyes. When the LORD giveth you rest, there shall be a place which the LORD shall choose to cause His name to dwell. Thither bring all that I command. Rejoice before the LORD, ye, your sons, daughters, servants, the Levite within thy gates — he hath no part with you. Guidance: 'Every man what is right in his own eyes' — the worst sentence in scripture, written here as a warning before it becomes a chorus in Judges. Bring the Levite to the table. Worship is family-sized, not me-sized.

Take heed that thou offer not thy burnt offerings in every place that thou seest. Only in the place which the LORD shall choose in one of thy tribes, there shalt thou offer, do all I command. Guidance: 'Every place that thou seest' — the eye picks scenic ridges; God picks one. Convenience and beauty are not the criteria. Where God's name is put, that is where worship goes.

Take heed that thou offer not thy burnt offerings in every place that thou seest. Only in the place which the LORD shall choose in one of thy tribes, there shalt thou offer, do all I command. Thou mayest kill and eat flesh in all thy gates, whatsoever thy soul lusteth after. Only ye shall not eat the blood; pour it on the earth as water. The tithe, firstlings, vows, freewill offerings — eat these before the LORD thy God in the place which He shall choose. Forsake not the Levite within thy gates, as long as thou livest. Guidance: God distinguishes between ordinary meat (eat at home) and holy meat (eat at His table). Blood always belongs to Him, never to your plate. And one tender clause: do not forget the Levite. The landless tribe is the heart's test.

When the LORD shall enlarge thy border, thou shalt say: I will eat flesh. If the chosen place is too far, kill of thy herd as I have commanded; eat in thy gates whatsoever thy soul lusteth after. Only be sure that thou eat not the blood: for the blood is the life. Pour it on the earth as water. Guidance: Distance from the tabernacle does not mean carnivore restrictions — God allows the country dweller to enjoy meat at home. But the blood line is absolute. The blood is the life; only God owns life. Drain it, and dinner is yours.

Thy holy things and thy vows, thou shalt take to the place the LORD shall choose. Offer thy burnt offerings, flesh and blood, upon the altar; the blood shall be poured out upon the altar, thou shalt eat the flesh. Observe these words, that it may go well with thee, and with thy children after thee for ever, when thou doest that which is good and right in the sight of the LORD. Guidance: Holy things go to the holy place; common things stay home. The pilgrimage was God's way of keeping worship from going stale at every backyard altar. 'It may go well with thee and thy children' — obedience is generational warmth.

When the LORD shall cut off the nations, take heed lest thou be snared by following them; enquire not after their gods, saying, How did these nations serve their gods? even so will I do likewise. Thou shalt not do so unto the LORD: for every abomination which He hateth have they done unto their gods; even their sons and daughters they have burnt in the fire. Thou shalt not add to my command, nor diminish from it. Guidance: Cultural curiosity becomes a trap. 'How do they worship their gods?' — for a people who burn their children, the answer is a horror. Don't import the ritual; don't invent new ones. Add nothing, take nothing. Scripture is enough.
